#### The grants are organized into three categories:

1.   **Open Calls**: Current grant and opportunities with a deadline. Grants are listed by closing date.

2.   **Rolling Applications**: current grant and opportunities with rolling applications (but it’s still best to submit as early as possible).

3.   **Long term planning:** Grants that have closed their current rounds, but are expected to open new windows.

_This post is for paid subscribers. This helps support the time and effort it takes to curate and organize these opportunities._

* * *

### Open Calls:

> **Research-Industry Grants for International Collaboration, [The Australian Academy of Technological Sciences and Engineering (ATSE)](https://www.atse.org.au/)**. _*Closing Soon!*_

Seeking applications from Australian organizations collaborating on key areas of science and technology with priority partner countries across the Asia-Pacific region. Applicants must focus on one of five priority themes and include at least one priority partner country. Priority themes: advanced manufacturing; quantum computing; artificial intelligence; hydrogen production; RNA (including mRNA) vaccines and therapies. Priority countries: Brazil; Indonesia; Japan; Republic of Korea; Malaysia; New Zealand; Singapore; Thailand; Vietnam

*   **Geographies:**Australia and Priority countries: Brazil, Indonesia, Japan, Republic of Korea, Malaysia, New Zealand, Singapore, Thailand, Vietnam.

*   **Who can apply:** Project must be led by Australian organization, including for-profit or non-profit, Publicly Funded Research Organisation (PFRO), and Cooperative Research Centre.

*   **Funding amount:** AUD $100,000 - AUD $1M. Total of $6.3M available.

*   **Targeted Sectors/ SDGs: Innovation, research**

*   **Deadline: May 4, 2025 for Expressions of Interest.**

*   **[Learn more and apply here.](https://www.glodip.org.au/)**

* * *

> **2025 THRIVE Global Impact Challenge, [SVG Ventures](https://thriveagrifood.com/).**_*Closing this month!*_

The THRIVE Global Impact Challenge is on a mission to discover the most innovative startups tackling the biggest challenges in agri-food systems. This year, they are seeking groundbreaking solutions across six key themes driving transformation in the agrifood industry: Regenerative Agriculture, Artificial Intelligence & Automation, Animal Health & Nutrition, Clean Energy & Climate Tech, Water & Waste Management, Post Harvest & FoodTech. Three finalists from each of the six themes will be selected to pitch at the THRIVE Global Impact Summit in Silicon Valley, and the winning company will receive the THRIVE Global Impact Challenge Award in addition to investment from SVG Ventures up to $1M USD.

*   **Geographies:**Global.

*   **Who can apply:** Early-stage startups or entrepreneurs at the seed to Series A stage.

*   **Funding amount:** Winner will receive an investment from SVG Ventures up to $1M USD.

*   **Targeted Sectors/ SDGs:**Agriculture, food, innovation, climate, energy, AI.

*   **Deadline: May 25, 2025.**

*   **[Learn more and apply here.](https://thriveagrifood.com/thrive-global-impact-challenge-2025/)**

* * *

> **Prize for Cities 2025-2026: Catalyzing Healthy Cities, [WRI Ross Center for Sustainable Cities.](https://www.wri.org/cities?ap3c=IGfTExI0Kk0HyroGAGfTExKV8Wt_Y01nltrEKlQqad_MO3EHhw)**_*Closing this month!*_

The WRI Ross Center Prize for Cities recognizes trailblazing projects and initiatives for their contribution to inclusive and sustainable urban transformation. It seeks to inspire urban change-makers across the globe by amplifying lessons and telling impactful stories of transformative change. This Prize cycle invites submissions from projects leading the way in strengthening livability, nature and cities together for healthy, accessible and thriving communities.

*   **Geographies:**Global.

*   **Who can apply:** Businesses, NGOs, community groups, local governments and collaborations of all types of organizations and individuals from the public, private and not-for-profit sectors may apply.

*   **Funding amount:** $250,000 will be awarded to one grand prize winner and $25,000 each to four runners up.

*   **Targeted Sectors/ SDGs:**Cities, urban environments, innovation, health.

*   **Deadline: May 28, 2025.**

*   **[Learn more and apply here.](https://prizeforcities.org/)**

* * *

> **AWS Imagine Grant Program, Amazon Web Services (AWS)**. _*New!*_

# Global Smart City Partnership Program

## **With Whom Do We Collaborate?**

GSCP links four players in the Global Smart City network:

World Bank project teams assist client cities with a range of projects, from lending operations to advisory services. GPSC matches the technical experts with project teams; the experts then work as consultants with the teams, bringing technical know-how for smart-city design and implementation. The program also incorporates leading smart cities’ experiences through knowledge-sharing and peer-to-peer learning.

#### Korea as a Pioneer and Promoter of Smart Cities

#### Smart and Sustainable Cities - The Netherlands

#### Global Programs and Partnerships on Smart Cities

## How Do We Collaborate?

![Image](/content/dam/photos/780x439/2021/aug-2/GSCP-4-Players.png)

## Partners

Partnerships with leading smart cities and global technical experts are at the core of GSCP. The program fosters connection and collaboration among World Bank Group teams and clients, smart city experts, and partners.

**[MOLIT Ministry of Land, Infrastructure and Transport](https://smartcity.go.kr/en/%E2%80%AF)** promotes Korea's smart city development efforts and seek global cooperation in this realm through K-City Network - a smart city national policy information portal. Global Cooperation Program is a government-to-government cooperation program which is launched by MOLIT in 2020 to share Korea’s smart city development expertise and experience. The program provides funding and technical support for basic study development for a smart city project like a master plan or feasibility study and a short-term training course in Korea for the host government officials. It also offers three sub-programs for recipient countries to choose from based on their needs.

[**The Korea Research Institute for Human Settlements (KRIHS)**](https://www.krihs.re.kr/eng/) stands as the pioneer think tank in Korea, committed to conducting comprehensive analyses of policies governing the efficient utilization, development, and conservation of land resources. Since its establishment in 1978, the KRIHS has been dedicated to advancing the balanced development of the national territory, enhancing our nation's competitiveness, promoting sustainable development, and elevating the quality of life for our citizens.

**[The Netherlands Enterprise Agency](https://english.rvo.nl/)** (Rijksdienst voor Ondernemend Nederland, RVO) is an executive body of the Dutch Ministry of Economic Affairs and Climate Policy (Ministerie van Economische Zaken en Klimaat, EZK). It helps entrepreneurs, NGOs, knowledge institutes, policymakers and organizations to invest, develop and expand their businesses and projects both in the Netherlands and abroad. It provides a platform to improve collaborations and strengthen positions through funding and networks.

**[Amsterdam Smart City](https://amsterdamsmartcity.com/%E2%80%AF)** is an open innovation platform that brings together innovation professionals from governments, companies, knowledge institutions and civil society organizations to shape the city and region of the future. They offer a meeting place and (online) workspace for metropolitan challenges and solutions. Via this online community, innovation and sustainability professionals share their smart city updates, opportunities, challenges, projects and events.

**[Tomorrow.City](https://tomorrow.city/welcome)** is a digital content platform that combines knowledge, business and awareness to accelerate sustainable and inclusive cities for all. It’s a global hub to help advance the smart city industry by connecting cities with companies, driving talent development and building momentum for sustainable and inclusive urban projects all around the world.

![GSCP Logo](/content/dam/photos/480x225/2021/aug/GSCP-Logo-Words-Horiz.png "GSCP Logo")

### Contact Us

## Other resources

**[The Smart City Hub Switzerland Association](https://www.smartcityhub.ch/home.4en.html)** serves to promote cooperation and the exchange of knowledge. The declared aim of the Association is to promote cooperation and the exchange of knowledge between Swiss cities and their businesses, service providers close to the federal government, as well as federal agencies and research in the Smart City area. It is intended to enable its members to use synergies and to share the knowledge and skills required for this purpose. The central task is to support the implementation of projects, in particular to save time and costs. Further tasks are the promotion of dialogue and cooperation, the development of innovative solutions that are close to the citizen and customer, and the use of synergies and scalability.

**[Innosuisse](https://www.innosuisse.ch/inno/en/home/media-and-events/newsroom/ntn-innovation-booster-new-initiatives.html)** is the Swiss Innovation Agency. Its role is to promote science-based innovation including smart city solutions in the interest of the economy and society in Switzerland. Innosuisse especially promotes the partnership between academia and the market with innovation projects, networking, training and coaching, laying the groundwork for successful Swiss start-ups as well as innovative products and services. It provides considerable benefits for a prosperous and sustainable economy. Innosuisse is a federal entity under public law with a separate legal personality. It provides support in accordance with the subsidiarity principle: it only supports projects if the innovation could not be implemented and market potential would not be tapped into without funding.

**[The World Smart Sustainable Cities Organization (WeGO)](https://we-gov.org/regional-networks/)** is an international association of city and other local governments, smart tech solution providers, and national and regional institutions committed to the transformation of cities into smart sustainable cities. WeGO’s Secretariat is based in Seoul and supported by regional offices in Africa (Abuja, Nigeria), East Asia (Chengdu, China), Eurasia (Ulyanovsk Region, Russia), the Mediterranean (Beyoglu, Turkey), and Latin America (Mexico City, Mexico). WeGO has more than 200 members around the world and serves as their international platform to improve the quality of life, innovate in the delivery of public services, and strengthen regional competitiveness.

**[The Smart Cities Marketplace](https://smart-cities-marketplace.ec.europa.eu/)** was created by merging the two former European Commission projects “Marketplace of the European Innovation Partnership on Smart Cities and Communities” (EIP-SCC) and the “Smart Cities Information System” (SCIS) into one single platform. It is a major market-changing undertaking that aims to bring cities, industries, SMEs, investors, researchers and other smart city actors together. The Smart Cities Marketplace has thousands of followers from all over Europe and beyond, many of which have signed up as a member. Their common aims are to improve citizens’ quality of life, increase the competitiveness of European cities and industry as well as to reach European energy and climate targets.

**[The UNDP Global Centre for Technology, Innovation and Sustainable Development (GC-TISD)](https://www.undp.org/sgtechcentre)** is a hub for knowledge and policy relating to sustainable development, with the emphasis on connecting top policymakers, practitioners, think tanks, business and other relevant stakeholders for learning and sharing of best practices on the themes of technology, innovation, and partnerships for sustainable development. UNDP GC-TISD is also a platform to study and showcase successful examples of innovation and partnerships to achieve sustainable development, leveraging Singapore as a living lab for global sustainability solutions, UNDP’s global development advisory and implementation services network, and UNDP’s partnership-building efforts within the UN system and with the international financial institutions.

![World Bank](https://www.worldbank.org/ext/en/media_1540be393bca9ed1fbc89eaa862044ccb08019dd2.svg?width=750&format=svg&optimize=medium)

--- SOURCE 4: Smart City Funding Opportunities - Grant Professionals Association ---
URL: https://grantschool.grantprofessionals.org/courses/93127

SUMMARY:
This presentation will showcase the number of grant opportunities that can support widespread smart city initiatives.

FULL CONTENT:
# [Grant Professionals Association](/ "Grant Professionals Association")

![]()

#### Item Added to Cart

You just added

You have
item(s) in your cart

# Smart City Funding Opportunities

Description: This presentation will showcase the number of grant opportunities that can support widespread smart city initiatives. We will review specific grant program deadlines, priorities, award amounts, while outlining specific examples of awarded projects from cities of all sizes. We will offer tips and best practices to navigate the sheer volume of funding mechanisms from varying federal grantmaking agencies. Smart city initiatives can be funded through agencies such as the Department of Transportation, the Environmental Protection Agency, the Department of Commerce, among others. Attendees will learn specifically about the grantmaking agencies responsible for awarding funds that help influence smart city priorities in order to best prepare for upcoming programs. While learning about the different types of projects that have been funded, attendees will better grasp the programs that will help make smart city plans a reality. In addition to diving into already established programs, attendees will learn about relevant upcoming federal priorities based on passed legislation. Given the heavy focus on federal funding opportunities, this session best fits within the "Federal Grants" Learning Track. Learning ObjectivesHow to research, identify, and match funding resources to meet specific needs. Attendees will be able to analyze different grantmaking organizations in order to find opportunities that fit their project needs.   
Organizational development as it pertains to grant seeking ""“ Attendees will be able to better understand various upcoming deadlines for smart city inspired opportunities.   
How to craft, construct, and submit an effective grant application ""“ Attendees will learn about varying priorities, award amounts, and deadlines for smart city initiatives.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Encouraging investments in R&D can drive innovations in smart city technologies, harnessing AI and IoT solutions to enhance urban living and sustainability.
Increased funding for smart technologies, such as renewable energy and smart infrastructure, can improve urban efficiency, reduce carbon footprints, and foster economic growth.
Engaging citizens in smart city planning ensures their needs are met, fostering transparency and collaboration, ultimately leading to more successful and sustainable urban solutions.
